title: Zhuangzi, Chapter 5: The Sign of Virtue Complete
description: Chapter 5 of the Zhuangzi — the Taoist masterpiece of paradox and parable.
introduction: Chapter 5 of the Zhuangzi — the Taoist masterpiece of paradox and parable.

translation.text: In the state of Lu there was a man named Wang Tai who had had his foot cut off. He had as many followers as Confucius. He stood without teaching. He sat without discussing. Empty-headed, his followers came; full-headed, they returned. So there must be some teaching that has no words.
